what_it_does: Seam-lane report (JSON record) for the `rule` tool consolidation in mcp_face/tools/rule.py, folding flat tools attach_rule/dry_run_rule/validate_rule into one tool with op=validate|dry_run|attach|detach. Records that the consolidation was already done in a prior commit and that this beat made no edits, plus verification and floor reasoning.
observations: Ops [validate, dry_run, attach, detach]; flat_tools_to_remove lists the three originals.

how_verified describes isolated harnesses (/tmp/verify_rule_lane.py and /tmp/verify_rule_attach.py) building a throwaway FastMCP plus real Suite, confirming only ['rule'] registers, no model/GPU needed.
States rule ops are pure grammar/AST work; validate/dry_run are pure reads, attach/detach surface a role_build proposal that never self-applies.

Notes this is an ADOPTION lane, not net-new: consolidation landed in git commit 016cf08 ('MCP-SWEEP 4 of 5 lanes') and the three flat tools were already removed; server.py:654 is a consolidation-marker comment.
Adds a `detach` op beyond the spec — the symmetric remove of attach.
Cites Suite methods at runtime/suite.py:9261/9291/9307/9324 and reproduces flat-tool value conversions exactly (destination or None / destination or 'inject').
Notes runtime/rules.py FORBIDDEN_DESTINATION_VERBS=('resolve','approve','dispatch') rejects forbidden verbs at build_rule construction.
standouts: Idempotency posture: reports nothing left to remove, flat_tools_to_remove kept only for completeness.
Floor held 'by construction' — forbidden destination verbs rejected at grammar-construction time.
Made zero edits this beat after confirming the existing module already met and exceeded the lane spec.
conventions: Same seam-lane report schema as sibling lanes.
Cross-checks wrapper signatures against both live Suite methods and original flat-tool git bodies.
notes: An adoption/idempotency lane report confirming the `rule` tool was already consolidated in an earlier commit and adding a symmetric detach op, with the route-floor enforced at grammar construction.
questions: If the consolidation already shipped in 016cf08, what triggered this lane to be re-run as a no-edit beat?
Were the /tmp verification scripts intended to be discarded, given they live outside the repo?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it presents as the rule-lane report and accurately documents an already-complete consolidation plus a verification re-run.
intention_signals: Documents catching that a lane's work was already shipped, re-proving it still holds, and extending it with the symmetric detach rather than redoing it.
novelty: The detach op as a symmetric complement to attach, and the no-edit-but-still-verified beat treating prior commit work as the deliverable.
contribution: Contributes the `rule` tool's op surface (validate/dry_run/attach/detach), the construction-time forbidden-verb enforcement detail, and the symmetric detach op — distinct from the other folded tools' surfaces.
summary_for_embedding: Seam-lane report confirming the `rule` MCP tool was already consolidated (commit 016cf08) into op=validate|dry_run|attach, re-verified in isolation, and extended with a symmetric detach op.
intention_for_embedding: To adopt and re-verify an already-shipped rule-tool consolidation and add the symmetric detach op while keeping the route floor enforced at grammar construction.
